author: ccmdi
description: Schema enforcement for frontmatter. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.
name: Propsec

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Added "exists" and "not exists" operators for property conditions
- Field conditions now support OR logic in addition to AND
- Added a global exclusions setting that lets you exclude notes from all schemas at once (e.g., archived notes or templates)
- Added an export button in settings to save all your schemas and custom types to a JSON file
- Schema and type preview modals now show a copy button to quickly copy the configuration as JSON to your clipboard
- Type preview modals now display usage statistics showing how many schemas and notes reference that type
- Constraint editing moved from floating overlays to inline accordions that expand within the field card
- Fields that share the same name (union variants) are now visually grouped together with an accent border
- Fields with conditions now show a small filter icon badge so you can see at a glance which fields have conditional logic
- The value input for conditions is automatically hidden when using exists/not exists operators since no comparison value is needed
- The violations panel no longer shows a placeholder message when there are zero violations
- Renamed "propertyEquals" to "conditions" in the README to match the actual feature name
- Added example images to the README

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
* Internal style name changes
* ESLint compliance/type safety
* Minor UI text adjustment

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- **File name filtering**: Schemas can now match files based on their name using regex patterns (e.g., `^Project-.+` to match files starting with "Project-")
- **Custom type handling fix**: Editing a custom type now automatically re-validates all schemas that use it, including schemas that reference it indirectly
- **Better unique constraint detection**: Unique field checking now works incrementally and correctly updates all affected files when duplicates are found
- **Middle-click behavior fix**: Middle-clicking file links in the violations panel no longer triggers unwanted auto-scroll

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
* Added cross-field validation (e.g. is start date < end date)
* Added min/max constraints on dates
* Added "unique" flag to fields - errors on duplicates
* Queries now support **AND, OR, and NOT**
* Frontmatter lookups optimized to O(1) for validating fields

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Added a validation cache to significantly reduce validation time (e.g. on startup). Validations now load optimistically on startup from this cache and reconcile with new state as it arrives.
- Microtasks for UI updates are coalesced to save on renders (such as in the violation view) - performance improvement

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Significant validation performance improvements (query index + yield to main)
- Moved to **validation accumulation** (file can be validated against multiple schemas at the same time) - per-schema grouping of violations to fix overwrites of lower schemas
- Property suggester on more fields, and supports proper casing
- Violation sidebar view has state persistence
- Significant rendering performance improvements for violation sidebar - implements incremental loading
- Smaller style bundle size

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Initial release includes:
- Schema and custom type support
- All primitive types
- Require/warn per-field flag
- Nested validation
- Violation sidebar and modal (with search and tab filters)
- Queries for schema matching (folder/tag)
- Property filters for schema matching
- Preview modal for "computed schema" view
- Draggable schema/type ordering
- Auto-detect template folder
- Settings for validation/UI preferences

...and more.
